response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional software engineering leadership and seniority, they fundamentally lack the core requirement: substantial C++ expertise. Only 6 months of C++ experience in 2014 is insufficient for a senior C++ developer role, and the subsequent 11-year career trajectory focuses entirely on web development (TypeScript/Node.js). Additionally, there is no demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IoT systems, secure development lifecycle practices, IEC 62443 standards, or industrial cybersecurity—all critical for this manufacturing-focused role. The technical ramp-up would be extensive.
strengths: Proven staff/senior-level technical leadership with strong mentoring record, architectural contributions, and consistent progression across multiple organizations
Extensive expertise in Node.js and React (advantageous skills), plus demonstrated event-driven architecture experience and real-time system optimization (Dunnhumby async I/O, caching)
Excellent software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD pipelines, and open-source contributions demonstrating quality mindset and communication skills
concerns: Negligible C++ experience (6 months at National Instruments in 2014, 11 years ago) despite it being the primary technical requirement; recent decade-long focus on TypeScript/Node.js web development

No background in industrial automation, manufacturing, IoT systems, or industrial cybersecurity standards; experience is in consumer platforms, GIS applications, and direct-mail services, not control systems or machinery

No evidence of secure development lifecycle practices, compliance frameworks (IEC 62443), message brokers, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850); education is in CS and Fine Arts, not engineering

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise depth (6 months vs. 8-12 years required)
Industrial automation and cybersecurity domain experience
Senior technical leadership and architecture understanding
